seoTitle: How to Pause Cloudflare to Stop It Blocking a Site (2026)
metaDescription: If Cloudflare is wrongly blocking visitors, pause it site-wide or switch specific records to DNS Only to route traffic straight to your origin.
intro: If Cloudflare's security layer is mistakenly blocking legitimate visitors, pausing it gives you a quick way to restore access while you investigate. This guide covers pausing the whole zone, or unproxying just the affected records instead.

steps.heading: Open your domain overview
steps.body: In Cloudflare, click into the specific website domain that's affected.

steps.heading: Find the Advanced Actions panel
steps.body: Scroll down the overview screen to locate the pause control.

steps.heading: Pause Cloudflare on Site
steps.body: Click the pause link and confirm the security warning, noting the roughly five-minute delay before it fully applies.

steps.heading: Or unproxy specific records instead
steps.body: If you only want to stop blocking for certain paths, go to DNS Records and switch relevant entries to the gray-cloud 'DNS Only' mode instead of pausing everything.

steps.heading: Verify traffic is unblocked
steps.body: Refresh the overview page to confirm the paused status, then run a terminal ping test to confirm requests reach your origin server directly.

faq.question: Should I pause the whole domain or just unproxy one record?
faq.answer: Pausing affects your entire zone at once, while switching a single record to DNS Only lets you resolve a specific blocking issue without losing protection elsewhere.

faq.question: Will pausing Cloudflare fix a false-positive security block right away?
faq.answer: Yes, since pausing stops the proxy from evaluating traffic, but expect roughly a five-minute window before the change is fully live.

faq.question: What should I check on my origin server while Cloudflare is paused?
faq.answer: This is a good window to run diagnostics directly against your server to confirm it's healthy and responding correctly without Cloudflare in front of it.

faq.question: Does pausing affect my DNS records permanently?
faq.answer: No, your DNS records and configuration stay intact; pausing only disables the proxy layer until you turn it back on.
